evals.prompt: I have a registration form with an age field. The user types their age as a whole number (integer). The valid age range is 18 to 60. Help me create test cases for this field.
evals.expected_output: Test case tables with BVA boundary values and/or business test data. Input column labeled with field name.
assertions: The output includes test case tables with Input column labeled with field name (e.g. 'Input: Age')
The output generates tables directly without asking for precision clarification

evals.prompt: Our booking system accepts appointments between 09:00 and 17:00. Help me create test cases for the time input.
evals.expected_output: Test case tables with time boundary values and Input column labeled with field name.
assertions: The output includes test case tables with Input column labeled with field name (e.g. 'Input: Time' or 'Input: Appointment Time')
The output includes time-related test data
The Expected Output column shows Valid or Invalid (or equivalent pass/fail)

evals.prompt: I have an input field for "comments". Help me create BVA test cases.
evals.expected_output: The skill recognizes that a free-text field lacks numeric or time boundaries and asks for clarification or suggests alternative techniques.
assertions: The output recognizes that a free-text field is not suitable for BVA
The output asks for clarification or suggests alternative testing techniques

evals.prompt: The score field must be at least 0. Generate BVA test cases.
evals.expected_output: The skill detects only a lower limit (minimum 0) with no upper limit, asks the user to confirm or provide the upper limit before generating test cases.
assertions: The output detects that only a lower limit (0) is provided
The output asks the user to provide the missing upper limit before generating test cases
The output does not generate a full BVA table without user confirmation

evals.prompt: The discount percentage cannot exceed 50%. Create BVA test cases for this field.
evals.expected_output: The skill detects only an upper limit (maximum 50) with no lower limit, asks the user to provide the missing lower limit before generating test cases.
assertions: The output asks the user for clarification or choices before generating test cases
The output does not generate a full BVA table without user confirmation

evals.prompt: Salary field accepts values from 15000 to 200000 (integer, Thai Baht). Generate BVA test cases with test data I can use immediately.
evals.expected_output: Test case tables with Input column labeled with field name, containing concrete salary values.
assertions: The output includes test case tables with Input column labeled with field name (e.g. 'Input: Salary')
The output contains concrete numeric salary values ready to copy-paste
The Expected Output column clearly indicates whether the system should accept or reject the value

evals.prompt: Registration requires users to be not over 65 years old. Create BVA test cases for age validation.
evals.expected_output: The skill asks the user to clarify the minimum unit of age data — is it measured in whole years only (e.g. age 64 vs 65 vs 66), or in years-months-days via birthdate (e.g. 64y 11m 29d)? This determines whether the input field is age-as-integer or birthdate.
assertions: The output presents options for age precision including whole years and birthdate for the user to choose from
The output explains how different precision levels lead to different boundary values
The output does not generate a final BVA table before the user confirms the precision

evals.prompt: Bank account has a daily deposit limit of not over 30,000 baht. Generate BVA test cases for the deposit amount field.
evals.expected_output: The skill asks the user to clarify money precision before generating test cases.
assertions: The output asks for clarification or presents choices before generating test cases
The output does not generate a final BVA table before the user confirms the precision

evals.prompt: Registration form requirements: user must be not over 65 years old, and the registration fee is not over 5,000 baht. Generate BVA test cases.
evals.expected_output: The skill detects two fields needing clarification and asks user to clarify before generating test cases.
assertions: The output asks for clarification about multiple fields before generating test cases
The output does not generate BVA tables before the user answers the questions

evals.prompt: Registration requires users to be not over 65 years old. The user enters their birthdate and the system calculates age. The valid range is 18 to 65. Generate BVA test cases.
evals.expected_output: Test case tables with Input columns using 'Input:' prefix for direct and indirect inputs, showing birthdates and transaction dates.
assertions: The output includes test case tables with 'Input:' prefixed columns for birthdate and/or transaction date
The output explains or demonstrates the relationship between birthdate, transaction date, and calculated age

evals.prompt: I have a registration form with an age field. The user types their age as a whole number (integer). The valid age range is 18 to 60. This is for a car insurance application targeting working adults. Help me create test cases for this field.
evals.expected_output: Two separate tables: (1) Unit Test Cases with BVA boundary values 17, 18, 19, 59, 60, 61, and (2) Business Test Cases with realistic ages from the business context (e.g. 25 fresh graduate, 35 mid-career, 55 pre-retirement).
assertions: The output contains two separate tables: one for unit test cases and one for business test cases
The unit test cases table includes BVA boundary values (17, 18, 19, 59, 60, 61)
The business test cases table includes realistic ages based on the business context (e.g. typical car insurance customer ages)
The business test cases have descriptions that reference the business domain (insurance, working adults, or similar context)

evals.prompt: Salary field accepts values from 15000 to 200000 (integer, Thai Baht). This is for a personal loan application. Most applicants earn between 20000-50000 baht. Generate test cases.
evals.expected_output: Two separate tables: (1) Unit Test Cases with BVA boundaries, and (2) Business Test Cases with realistic salary values based on Thai loan applicant demographics.
assertions: The output contains two separate tables: unit test cases and business test cases
The unit test cases table includes BVA boundary values (14999, 15000, 15001, 199999, 200000, 200001)
The business test cases table includes realistic salary values from the business context (e.g. 20000, 25000, 30000, 50000 for typical Thai loan applicants)
The business test cases are clearly labeled as being for acceptance or integration level testing

evals.prompt: I have a quantity field accepting 1 to 500 (integer). Generate test cases.
evals.expected_output: The skill generates unit test cases and either asks about business context or generates generic business test data with an offer to customize.
assertions: The output asks the user about business context OR generates business test data with an offer to customize for a specific domain
The output does not only produce BVA boundary values — it addresses business test data in some way

evals.prompt: Personal loan application: applicant age must be 20-60 (integer) AND monthly salary must be 15000-200000 (integer, Thai Baht). Generate test cases.
evals.expected_output: The skill analyzes each condition separately with BVA, then combines them into one test scenario table. Each test case tests one boundary condition at a time while keeping the other field at a valid (nominal) value. The table has Input columns for both fields.
assertions: The output identifies two separate conditions (age and salary) and analyzes each with BVA
The output produces a combined test scenario table with Input columns for both fields (e.g. Input: Age and Input: Salary)
When testing age boundaries, the salary value is set to a valid nominal value (not a boundary)
When testing salary boundaries, the age value is set to a valid nominal value (not a boundary)
The combined table covers BVA boundaries for both conditions

evals.prompt: Insurance policy: customer age 25-65 (integer), coverage amount 100000-5000000 (integer, Thai Baht), and policy duration 1-30 years (integer). Generate test cases.
evals.expected_output: The skill analyzes three conditions, applies BVA to each, and combines into one test scenario table with three Input columns. Each test case tests one field's boundary while other fields use valid nominal values.
assertions: The output identifies three separate conditions (age, coverage amount, duration)
The output produces a combined test scenario table with Input columns for all three fields
Each test case varies only one field's boundary while keeping other fields at valid nominal values
The combined table covers BVA boundaries for all three conditions
